title: Titanic
text: Titanic had a real iceberg… sort of. The iceberg scene wasn't only CGI; James Cameron built a real 15-ton replica for the actors to interact with.

title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
text: Dobby's voice in Harry Potter was tricky at first. The actor had only a few lines to record, but the takes were too fast and high-pitched, so it took months to make them usable.

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
text: Peter Jackson used forced perspective to make Hobbits look smaller without CGI — a classic cinematic illusion.

title: The Lion King
text: The Lion King is essentially an adaptation of Shakespeare's Hamlet — Scar is even the animal kingdom's Claudius.

title: Gladiator
text: A modern car appears in the background of one Gladiator scene — one of the most famous "goofs" fans love to spot.

title: Mission Impossible
text: Tom Cruise has performed stunts so dangerous he's broken bones and dislocated a shoulder on set. A real-life action hero.

title: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope
text: David Prowse physically played Darth Vader, but the voice was James Earl Jones — Prowse didn't hear the final voice until the premiere.

title: Psycho
text: The famous shower scene in Psycho was shot over 7 days with 70 camera angles — the "blood" was chocolate syrup.
